title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: tan‚Ā°őł+cot‚Ā°őł\tan \theta + \cot \thetatanőł+cotőł‚Äč = 2 and őł‚ąą(0,ŌÄ/2)\theta \in (0, \pi/2)őł‚ąą(0,ŌÄ/2),that what is sec‚Ā°őł+cosec‚Ā°őł\sec \theta + \cosec \thetasecőł+cosecőł = ?
questions.explanation: Given ‚Äčtan‚Ā°őł+cot‚Ā°őł=2\tan \theta + \cot \theta = 2tanőł+cotőł=2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: If tan‚Ā°őł+1tan‚Ā°őł=2\tan \theta + \frac{1}{\tan \theta} = 2tanőł+tanőł1‚Äč=2‚Äč, then tan‚Ā°őł=1\tan \theta = 1tanőł=1‚Äč‚Äč Thus, őł=45‚ąė \theta = 45^\circőł=45‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčsec‚Ā°45‚ąė+csc‚Ā°45‚ąė=2+2=22\sec 45^\circ + \csc 45^\circ = \sqrt{2} + \sqrt{2} = 2\sqrt{2}sec45‚ąė+csc45‚ąė=2‚Äč+2‚Äč=22‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč answer=(a)

questions.question: Which of the following is correct? ‚Äč10+3>8+5 10+3<8+5 10+3=8+5\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} > \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}\\ \ \\\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} < \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}\\ \ \\\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} = \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}10‚Äč+3‚Äč>8‚Äč+5‚Äč 10‚Äč+3‚Äč<8‚Äč+5‚Äč 10‚Äč+3‚Äč=8‚Äč+5‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: Case (i): 10+3>8+5\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} > \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}10‚Äč+3‚Äč>8‚Äč+5‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Case (ii): 10+3<8+5\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} < \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}10‚Äč+3‚Äč<8‚Äč+5‚Äč Case (iii):10+3=8+5\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} = \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}10‚Äč+3‚Äč=8‚Äč+5‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Concept Used: To compare a+b\sqrt{a} + \sqrt{b}a‚Äč+b‚Äč‚Äč and c+d \sqrt{c} + \sqrt{d}c‚Äč+d‚Äč where a + b = c + d, compare the products ab and cd. Formula Used: ‚Äč(x+y)2=x2+y2+2xy(x + y)^2 = x^2 + y^2 + 2xy(x+y)2=x2+y2+2xy‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Square both sides: ‚Äč(10+3)2=10+3+230=13+230 (8+5)2=8+5+240=13+240 (\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3})^2 = 10 + 3 + 2\sqrt{30} = 13 + 2\sqrt{30}\\ \ \\(\sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5})^2 = 8 + 5 + 2\sqrt{40} = 13 + 2\sqrt{40}\\ \ \\(10‚Äč+3‚Äč)2=10+3+230‚Äč=13+230‚Äč (8‚Äč+5‚Äč)2=8+5+240‚Äč=13+240‚Äč Since 40>30\sqrt{40} > \sqrt{30}40‚Äč>30‚Äč‚Äč, then 8+5>10+3\sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5} > \sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3}8‚Äč+5‚Äč>10‚Äč+3‚Äč (True) Case (iii): 10+3=8+5\sqrt{10} + \sqrt{3} = \sqrt{8} + \sqrt{5}10‚Äč+3‚Äč=8‚Äč+5‚Äč (False)‚Äč Exam-Hall Method: Sum of terms is 13 for both. Product 10 √ó\times√ó‚Äč 3 = 30 and 8√ó \times√ó‚Äč 5 = 40 Higher product means higher value. So ii is correct. So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: Due to water crisis, 38% of the initial population left the town, and 50% of the remaining population left the town for pursuing wobs. If presently 4450 people inhabit the town, what is the ratio of initial population to present population?
questions.explanation: Given Initial migration = 38% Secondary migration = 50% of remaining Present population = 4450 Solution Let the initial population be 100x. 38% people left. Remaining = 100x - 38x = 62x 50% of the remaining left. Number leaving = 50% of 62x = 31x Remaining population = 62x - 31x = 31x We need the ratio of Initial Population to Present Population. Ratio = 100x : 31x = 100 : 31 Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: The length of each of two tangents drawn from an external point to a circle is 15 cm. What is the distance from the external point to the center of the circle, if the radius is 8 cm?
questions.explanation: Given: Tangent = 15, Radius = 8. Formula Used: ‚ÄčH2=P2+B2H^2 = P^2 + B^2H2=P2+B2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Distance = 152+82=225+64\sqrt{15^2 + 8^2} = \sqrt{225 + 64}152+82‚Äč=225+64‚Äč‚Äč = 17 cm. So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: What is the result of (0.73+0.23‚ąí0.93)√∑(3√ó0.7√ó0.2√ó(‚ąí0.9))(0.7^3 + 0.2^3 - 0.9^3) \div (3 \times 0.7 \times 0.2 \times (-0.9))(0.73+0.23‚ąí0.93)√∑(3√ó0.7√ó0.2√ó(‚ąí0.9))‚Äč = ?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äč(0.73+0.23‚ąí0.93)√∑(3√ó0.7√ó0.2√ó(‚ąí0.9))(0.7^3 + 0.2^3 - 0.9^3) \div (3 \times 0.7 \times 0.2 \times (-0.9))(0.73+0.23‚ąí0.93)√∑(3√ó0.7√ó0.2√ó(‚ąí0.9))‚Äč‚Äč Concept Used: If a + b + c = 0, then a3+b3+c3a^3 + b^3 + c^3a3+b3+c3‚Äč = 3abc Solution: 0.7 + 0.2 + (-0.9) = 0 Numerator = 3(0.7)(0.2)(-0.9) Denominator = 3(0.7)(0.2)(-0.9) Now divide, Result = 1 Exam-Hall Method: Recognize identity. 3abc / 3abc = 1 So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: If x+y=7\sqrt{x} + \sqrt{y} = 7x‚Äč+y‚Äč=7‚Äč and x‚ąíy=3\sqrt{x} - \sqrt{y} = 3x‚Äč‚ąíy‚Äč=3‚Äč, what is the value of x and y?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčx+y=7 x‚ąíy=3\sqrt{x} + \sqrt{y} = 7\\ \ \\\sqrt{x} - \sqrt{y} = 3x‚Äč+y‚Äč=7 x‚Äč‚ąíy‚Äč=3‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let x\sqrt{x}x‚Äč‚Äč = a and y\sqrt{y}y‚Äč‚Äč = b. The equations become: 1) a + b = 7 2) a - b = 3 Adding (1) and (2): 2a = 10 =&gt \Rightarrow=&gt‚Äč a = 5 Subtracting (2) from (1): 2b = 4 =&gt \Rightarrow=&gt‚Äč b = 2 Now, x=5=&gtx=52\sqrt{x} = 5 \Rightarrow x = 5^2x‚Äč=5=&gtx=52‚Äč = 25 And y=2=&gty=22\sqrt{y} = 2 \Rightarrow y = 2^2y‚Äč=2=&gty=22‚Äč = 4 So the correct answer is (d).

questions.question: A mutual fund‚Äôs value increases by 20% in the first year, remains unchanged in the second year and then increases by 25% in the third year. In the fourth year, its value declines by an unknown percentage such that the final value is exactly 12.5% above the initial investment. Calculate the unknown percentage decrease in the fourth year.
questions.explanation: Given Year 1 Increase = 20% Year 2 Increase = 0% Year 3 Increase = 25% Overall Increase = 12.5% Formula Used Final Value = Initial Value √ó(1¬Īr1)√ó(1¬Īr2)‚Ä¶\times (1 \pm r_1) \times (1 \pm r_2) \dots√ó(1¬Īr1‚Äč)√ó(1¬Īr2‚Äč)‚Ä¶‚Äč‚Äč Solution Let the initial investment be P. Let the decline in the 4th year be r%. According to the question: ‚ÄčP√ó(1+20100)√ó1√ó(1+25100)√ó(1‚ąír100)=P√ó(1+12.5100) 1.2√ó1√ó1.25√ó(1‚ąír100)=1.125 1.5√ó(1‚ąír100)=1.125 1‚ąír100=1.1251.5 1‚ąír100=11.2515=0.75 r100=1‚ąí0.75=0.25 r=25%P \times (1 + \frac{20}{100}) \times 1 \times (1 + \frac{25}{100}) \times (1 - \frac{r}{100}) = P \times (1 + \frac{12.5}{100})\\\ \\1.2 \times 1 \times 1.25 \times (1 - \frac{r}{100}) = 1.125\\\ \\1.5 \times (1 - \frac{r}{100}) = 1.125\\\ \\1 - \frac{r}{100} = \frac{1.125}{1.5}\\\ \\1 - \frac{r}{100} = \frac{11.25}{15} = 0.75\\\ \\\frac{r}{100} = 1 - 0.75 = 0.25\\\ \\r = 25\%P√ó(1+10020‚Äč)√ó1√ó(1+10025‚Äč)√ó(1‚ąí100r‚Äč)=P√ó(1+10012.5‚Äč) 1.2√ó1√ó1.25√ó(1‚ąí100r‚Äč)=1.125 1.5√ó(1‚ąí100r‚Äč)=1.125 1‚ąí100r‚Äč=1.51.125‚Äč 1‚ąí100r‚Äč=1511.25‚Äč=0.75 100r‚Äč=1‚ąí0.75=0.25 r=25%‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: 18¬≥ + 14¬≥ - 32¬≥ + 24192 is equal to:
questions.explanation: Given: Expression: 18¬≥ + 14¬≥ - 32¬≥ + 24192‚Äč Concept Used: If a + b + c = 0, then a3+b3+c3a^3 + b^3 + c^3a3+b3+c3‚Äč = 3abc Solution: Let a = 18, b = 14, c = -32 a + b + c = 18 + 14 - 32 = 0 a3+b3+c3a^3 + b^3 + c^3a3+b3+c3‚Äč = 3(18)(14)(-32) = -24192 Result = -24192 + 24192 = 0 Exam-Hall Method: Recognize a + b + c = 0 Cubes sum to 3abc. Result is 0. So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: The difference between two positive numbers is 2639. When the larger number is divided by the smaller number, the quotient is 2 and the remainder is 130. Find the greatest common multiple (HCF) of the two given numbers and 4745.
questions.explanation: Given Difference between numbers = 2639 Larger divided by Smaller: Quotient = 2, Remainder = 130 Formula Used Dividend = Divisor √ó Quotient + Remainder Solution Let the smaller number be S and the larger number be L. L - S = 2639 =&gt\Rightarrow=&gt‚Äč L = S + 2639 According to the division algorithm: L = 2S + 130 Equating the two values of $L$: S + 2639 = 2S + 130 S = 2639 - 130 = 2509 Now find L: L = 2509 + 2639 = 5148 We need the HCF of 5148, 2509, and 4745. Factorize 4745: ‚Äč4745=5√ó949=5√ó13√ó734745 = 5 \times 949 = 5 \times 13 \times 734745=5√ó949=5√ó13√ó73‚Äč‚Äč Now check if 13 or 73 divides 2509 and 5148. Check 13: 2509√∑13=1932509 \div 13 = 193 2509√∑13=193‚Äč(Divisible) 5148√∑13=3965148 \div 13 = 3965148√∑13=396‚Äč (Divisible) Check 73: 2509√∑73‚Čą34.32509 \div 73 \approx 34.32509√∑73‚Čą34.3‚Äč (Not divisible) Thus, the HCF is 13. Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A cylindrical vessel is melted and recast into several smaller cylinders whose height is 1/4th and radius is ¬Ĺ of the original. What fraction of the original volume is represented by one small cylinder?
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčrnew=12rold,hnew=14holdr_{new} = \frac{1}{2}r_{old}, h_{new} = \frac{1}{4}h_{old}rnew‚Äč=21‚Äčrold‚Äč,hnew‚Äč=41‚Äčhold‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: ‚ÄčV=ŌÄr2hV = \pi r^2 hV=ŌÄr2h‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ‚ÄčVnew=ŌÄ(r2)2(h4) Vnew=ŌÄ(r24)(h4) Vnew=116ŌÄr2h=116VoldV_{new} = \pi (\frac{r}{2})^2 (\frac{h}{4})\\ \ \\V_{new} = \pi (\frac{r^2}{4}) (\frac{h}{4})\\ \ \\V_{new} = \frac{1}{16} \pi r^2 h = \frac{1}{16} V_{old}Vnew‚Äč=ŌÄ(2r‚Äč)2(4h‚Äč) Vnew‚Äč=ŌÄ(4r2‚Äč)(4h‚Äč) Vnew‚Äč=161‚ÄčŌÄr2h=161‚ÄčVold‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč So the correct answer is (a).

questions.question: Aman distributed 250 chocolates among four kids such that the share of the first kid, twice the share of the second kid, thrice the share of the third kid and four times the share of the fourth kid are all equal. Find the number of chocolates received by the first kid.
questions.explanation: ‚ÄčGiven: Total chocolates=250 Share of first kid, twice second, thrice third and four times fourth are equal Formula Used: Total=Sum of individual shares Solution: Let share of first kid=x Second kid share=x2 Third kid share=x3 Fourth kid share=x4 x+x2+x3+x4=250 12x+6x+4x+3x12=250 25x12=250 25x=3000 x=120 Final Answer: 120\textbf{Given:} \\ \, \\\text{Total chocolates} = 250 \\ \, \\\text{Share of first kid, twice second, thrice third and four times fourth are equal} \\ \, \\\textbf{Formula Used:} \\ \, \\\text{Total} = \text{Sum of individual shares} \\ \, \\\textbf{Solution:} \\ \, \\\text{Let share of first kid} = x \\ \, \\\text{Second kid share} = \frac{x}{2} \\ \, \\\text{Third kid share} = \frac{x}{3} \\ \, \\\text{Fourth kid share} = \frac{x}{4} \\ \, \\x + \frac{x}{2} + \frac{x}{3} + \frac{x}{4} = 250 \\ \, \\\frac{12x + 6x + 4x + 3x}{12} = 250 \\ \, \\\frac{25x}{12} = 250 \\ \, \\25x = 3000 \\ \, \\x = 120 \\ \, \\\textbf{Final Answer:} \\ \, \\120Given:Total chocolates=250Share of first kid, twice second, thrice third and four times fourth are equalFormula Used:Total=Sum of individual sharesSolution:Let share of first kid=xSecond kid share=2x‚ÄčThird kid share=3x‚ÄčFourth kid share=4x‚Äčx+2x‚Äč+3x‚Äč+4x‚Äč=2501212x+6x+4x+3x‚Äč=2501225x‚Äč=25025x=3000x=120Final Answer:120‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: If a3+b3a^3 + b^3 a3+b3‚Äč= 91 and ab = 12, find the value of a + b.
questions.explanation: Given: a3+b3a^3 + b^3a3+b3‚Äč = 91 ab = 12 Formula Used: a3+b3=(a+b)3a^3 + b^3 = (a+b)^3 a3+b3=(a+b)3‚Äč- 3ab(a+b) Solution: Let a + b = k ‚Äč91=k3‚ąí3(12)(k)91 = k^3 - 3(12)(k)91=k3‚ąí3(12)(k)‚Äč‚Äč k3k^3k3‚Äč - 36k - 91 = 0 Testing options for k: If k = 7 737^3 73‚Äč- 36(7) - 91 = 343 - 252 - 91 = 0 Therefore, a + b = 7 So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: A man sold an article for ‚āĻ697 after giving two successive discounts on its marked price of ‚āĻ2,788. The first discount was d% of the marked price and the second discount was equal in rupees to the first discount amount. Find the value of d.
questions.explanation: Given MP = 2788, SP = 697 1st Discount = d% of MP 2nd Discount Amount = 1st Discount Amount Solution Let the amount of the first discount be X. Price after 1st discount = 2788 - X The second discount amount is also X. Price after 2nd discount = (2788 - X) - X = 2788 - 2X Given SP = 697 2788 - 2X = 697 2X = 2788 - 697 2X = 2091 X = 1045.5 Now, calculate d (percentage): d = 1045.52788√ó100 \frac{1045.5}{2788} \times 10027881045.5‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč d = 0.375√ó1000.375 \times 1000.375√ó100‚Äč‚Äč d = 37.5% Final Answer So the correct answer is (b)

questions.question: If P, Q, and R are three amounts of money such that Q is the simple interest on P, and R is the simple interest on Q, all for the same time and at the same rate of interest, then which of the following is true?
questions.explanation: Given: Q is SI on P. R is SI on Q. Same R and T. Concept Used: SI is directly proportional to the principal for fixed R and T. Formula Used: ‚ÄčSI=PRT100SI = \frac{PRT}{100}SI=100PRT‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Q = PRT100=&gtRT100=QP\frac{PRT}{100} \Rightarrow \frac{RT}{100} = \frac{Q}{P}100PRT‚Äč=&gt100RT‚Äč=PQ‚Äč ‚Äč‚Äč R = QRT100=&gtRT100=RQ\frac{QRT}{100} \Rightarrow \frac{RT}{100} = \frac{R}{Q}100QRT‚Äč=&gt100RT‚Äč=QR‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Equating both: QP=RQ=&gtQ2=PR\frac{Q}{P} = \frac{R}{Q} \Rightarrow Q^2 = PRPQ‚Äč=QR‚Äč=&gtQ2=PR‚Äč‚Äč So the correct answer is (a).

questions.question: In ‚Ė≥\triangle‚Ė≥‚Äč ABC, AD is the perpendicular bisector of BC. Are the triangles ABD and ACD congruent? If so, by what rule?
questions.explanation: Given: AD ‚ä•\perp‚ä•‚Äč BC, BD = DC ![](https://cdn010.adda247.com/de7260c5-d3cc-4c45-95db-9207812f0f5c.png) ‚ÄčSolution: BD = DC (Given) ‚Äč‚ą†\angle‚ą†‚Äč‚Äč ADB = ‚ą†\angle ‚ą†‚Äč‚ÄčADC = 90‚ąė90^\circ 90‚ąė‚Äč‚Äč AD = AD (Common) By the SAS rule, triangles are congruent. So the correct answer is (c).‚Äč

questions.question: A brand offers a trade discount of 18% on its products to retailers. Additionally, during a promotional scheme, a 9% extra discount is provided. If the product is listed at ‚āĻ25,000, how much does a retailer pay?
questions.explanation: Given List Price = ‚āĻ25,000 Discount 1 = 18% Discount 2 = 9% Formula Used Selling Price = List Price √ó(1‚ąíd1100)√ó(1‚ąíd2100) \times (1 - \frac{d_1}{100}) \times (1 - \frac{d_2}{100})√ó(1‚ąí100d1‚Äč‚Äč)√ó(1‚ąí100d2‚Äč‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč Solution SP = 25000√ó(1‚ąí0.18)√ó(1‚ąí0.09) 25000 \times (1 - 0.18) \times (1 - 0.09)25000√ó(1‚ąí0.18)√ó(1‚ąí0.09)‚Äč‚Äč SP = 25000√ó0.82√ó0.91 25000 \times 0.82 \times 0.9125000√ó0.82√ó0.91‚Äč = 18655 Final Answer So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A right-angled triangle has legs of 9 cm and 12 cm. A second right-angled triangle is similar to the first, and its hypotenuse is 45 cm. What is the area of the second triangle?
questions.explanation: Given: T1 legs = 9, 12 T2 hypotenuse = 45 Concept Used: Scale factor in similar triangles. Formula Used: Area ratio = (Scale factor) ¬≤ Solution: T1 hyp = 92+122\sqrt{9^2 + 12^2}92+122‚Äč‚Äč = 15 Scale factor = 45 / 15 = 3 T1 Area = 0.5√ó9√ó120.5 \times 9 \times 12 0.5√ó9√ó12 = 54 cm¬≤ T2 Area =54√ó32=54√ó9 54 \times 3^2 = 54 \times 954√ó32=54√ó9‚Äč = 486 cm¬≤ So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: A rectangle is a quadrilateral in which:
options: All four interior angles are 90¬į and opposite sides are equal.
Diagonals intersect at 90¬į and bisect angles.
Only one pair of opposite sides is parallel.
All four sides are equal in length.
questions.explanation: A rectangle is defined by having four right angles and equal opposite sides. So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: Sneha lent ‚āĻ24,000 to Vikas on 4 April 2024 at a simple interest rate of 5% per annum. Vikas plans to pay back the entire amount on 16 June 2024. What is the total amount Vikas needs to return?
questions.explanation: Given: P = ‚āĻ24,000, R = 5%, Start = 4 April 2024, End = 16 June 2024. Concept Used: Interest is calculated for the number of days between two dates. Formula Used: ‚ÄčSI=P√óR√óT100SI = \frac{P \times R \times T}{100}SI=100P√óR√óT‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Days: April (26), May (31), June (16) = 73 days, (2024 is leap) ‚ÄčT=73366T = \frac{73}{366} T=36673‚Äč SI =24,000√ó5√ó1/5100 \frac{24,000 \times 5 \times ‚Öē}{100}10024,000√ó5√ó‚Öē‚Äč‚Äč = ‚āĻ240 Amount = 24,000 + 240 = ‚āĻ24,240. So the correct answer is (c)

questions.question: Water flows out through a circular pipe whose internal diameter is 2 cm, at the rate of 4 metres per second into a cylindrical tank, the radius of whose base is 80 cm. By how much will the level of water rise in 20 minutes?
questions.explanation: Given Pipe radius (r) = 1 cm Flow speed = 400 cm/s Time = 20 min = 1200 s Tank radius (R) = 80 cm Formula Used Volume In = Volume Rise in Tank ‚ÄčArea√óSpeed√óTime=ŌÄR2hArea \times Speed \times Time = \pi R^2 hArea√óSpeed√óTime=ŌÄR2h‚Äč‚Äč Solution Volume In = ŌÄ(1)2√ó400√ó1200=480000ŌÄ cm3\pi (1)^2 \times 400 \times 1200 = 480000 \pi \text{ cm}^3ŌÄ(1)2√ó400√ó1200=480000ŌÄ cm3‚Äč‚Äč Volume Rise = ŌÄ(80)2√óh=6400ŌÄh \pi (80)^2 \times h = 6400 \pi hŌÄ(80)2√óh=6400ŌÄh‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč480000ŌÄ=6400ŌÄhh=4800006400 h=480064 h=75 cm480000 \pi = 6400 \pi h \\ h = \frac{480000}{6400} \\\ \\ h = \frac{4800}{64}\\\ \\h = 75 \text{ cm}480000ŌÄ=6400ŌÄhh=6400480000‚Äč h=644800‚Äč h=75 cm‚Äč‚Äč Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A solid sphere is placed inside a cube such that it touches all six faces. What percentage of the cube's volume is occupied by the sphere?
questions.explanation: Given: Sphere diameter = Cube side (a). Concept Used: Volume comparison between inscribed sphere and cube. Formula Used: ‚Äč%=VsphereVcube√ó100\% = \frac{V_{sphere}}{V_{cube}} \times 100%=Vcube‚ÄčVsphere‚Äč‚Äč√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ‚ÄčVcube=a3 Vsphere=43ŌÄ(a2)3=ŌÄa36V_{cube} = a^3\\ \ \\V_{sphere} = \frac{4}{3}\pi \left(\frac{a}{2}\right)^3 = \frac{\pi a^3}{6}Vcube‚Äč=a3 Vsphere‚Äč=34‚ÄčŌÄ(2a‚Äč)3=6ŌÄa3‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Ratio = ŌÄ6‚Čą\frac{\pi}{6} \approx6ŌÄ‚Äč‚Čą‚Äč 0.5236. Percentage = 52.36%. So the correct answer is (d)

questions.question: What is the ratio of the area of a regular hexagon to the area of an equilateral triangle if both have the same side length?
questions.explanation: Given: A regular hexagon and an equilateral triangle have the same side length a. Find the ratio of their areas. Concept Used: A regular hexagon can be divided into 6 equilateral triangles of the same side length. Formula Used: Area of Hexagon = 6 √ó34a2\times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^2√ó43‚Äč‚Äča2‚Äč‚Äč Area of Triangle = 34a2 \frac{\sqrt{3}}{4} a^243‚Äč‚Äča2‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Ratio=6√ó(¬ĺ)a2(¬ĺ)a2 Ratio=6:1Ratio = \frac{6 \times (\sqrt{3}/4) a^2}{(\sqrt{3}/4) a^2} \\ \ \\Ratio = 6:1Ratio=(3‚Äč/4)a26√ó(3‚Äč/4)a2‚Äč Ratio=6:1‚Äč‚Äč So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: The sides of a rectangular prism are in the ratio 1:2:3. If the volume of the prism is 384 cm¬≥, what is the length of its longest side?
questions.explanation: Given: Ratio = 1:2:3, Volume = 384 cm^3. Concept Used: Volume of cuboid = l√ób√óh l \times b \times hl√ób√óh‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: V = (x)(2x)(3x) ‚Äč6x3=384=&gtx3=64=&gtx=4.6x^3 = 384 \Rightarrow x^3 = 64 \Rightarrow x = 4.6x3=384=&gtx3=64=&gtx=4.‚Äč‚Äč Longest side = 3x = 12 cm. So the correct answer is (c).

questions.question: Atul, Bharat, and Chetan enter into a partnership. Atul invests ‚āĻ25,000 for 6 months, Bharat invests ‚āĻ30,000 for 8 months, and Chetan invests ‚āĻ40,000 for 9 months. If the total profit is ‚āĻ37,000, what is Chetan's share of the profit?
questions.explanation: Given Atul: 25000 for 6 months Bharat: 30000 for 8 months Chetan: 40000 for 9 months Total Profit = 37000 Solution Ratio of shares = (InvA√óTA):(InvB√óTB):(InvC√óTC)(Inv_A \times T_A) : (Inv_B \times T_B) : (Inv_C \times T_C)(InvA‚Äč√óTA‚Äč):(InvB‚Äč√óTB‚Äč):(InvC‚Äč√óTC‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč Ratio = (25√ó6):(30√ó8):(40√ó9)(25 \times 6) : (30 \times 8) : (40 \times 9)(25√ó6):(30√ó8):(40√ó9)‚Äč‚Äč Ratio = 150 : 240 : 360 Divide by 30: Ratio = 5 : 8 : 12 Total parts = 5 + 8 + 12 = 25 Value of 1 part = 3700025=1480 \frac{37000}{25} = 14802537000‚Äč=1480‚Äč‚Äč Chetan's Share = 12√ó148012 \times 148012√ó1480‚Äč = 17760 Final Answer So the correct answer is (a)

questions.question: A refrigerator is priced at ‚āĻ45,000. A customer gets a discount of ‚āĻ5,000 on it. After the discount, the customer buys a voltage stabilizer for ‚āĻ2,000 at a 15% discount. What is the total amount the customer spends on both items?
questions.explanation: Given: Fridge Price = ‚āĻ45,000 Fridge Discount = ‚āĻ5,000 Stabilizer Price = ‚āĻ2,000 Stabilizer Discount = 15% Concept Used: Net Spend = (Price 1 - Discount 1) + (Price 2 - Discount 2) Formula Used: Selling Price = MP - (Discount%√óMP)100\frac{(Discount \% √ó MP)} { 100}100(Discount%√óMP)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Fridge Spend = 45,000 - 5,000 = ‚āĻ40,000 Stabilizer Discount = 15100√ó2000\frac{15}{100} \times 2000 10015‚Äč√ó2000‚Äč = ‚āĻ300 Stabilizer Spend = 2,000 - 300 = ‚āĻ1,700 Total Spend = 40,000 + 1,700 = ‚āĻ41,700 So the correct answer is (d).
